mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate sitting at an effective record low of 0.22% signals an advanced-stage monetary easing cycle that compresses the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or mean forward returns during low-rate periods are not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0% and continues rising for three consecutive months, labor market deterioration reaches a capitulation extreme that historically precedes equity market troughs, so forward 60-day S&P 500 returns turn positive as forward-looking markets price in eventual recovery ahead of labor data confirmation.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 8.0% and increases for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=8.1% asof t-1mo rising sharply from prior year
failure_condition: Average forward 60d return following triggered instances is not significantly positive (or is negative) across at least two independent recession episodes in the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint collapse of housing starts and retail sales indicates a broad-based consumer demand contraction that compounds risk-premium expansion beyond what either series alone implies, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations for consumer-facing sectors deteriorate simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: HOUST year-over-year decline exceeds 40% AND RSAFS month-over-month decline exceeds 1%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=466K near historic lows asof t-2mo
failure_condition: Predictive power (Rank IC) of the joint HOUST+RSAFS trigger is not superior to either single-series signal, or negative return incidence falls below 55% across triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month declines in industrial production during an active manufacturing recession regime signal continued output contraction, causing analysts to revise near-term earnings expectations downward and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Once the recession regime (identified via consecutive INDPRO declines) ends, the same trigger no longer produces negative mean 5-day returns, or the effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) within the recession window itself.

mechanism_narrative: A sequence of declining nonfarm payroll prints reflects deteriorating labor income momentum that inversely predicts short-horizon equity returns, as each weak print triggers incremental risk-off repositioning by investors over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and forward 5-day returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate reaches the effective zero lower bound (<=0.25%), markets anticipate unconventional monetary easing (e.g., quantitative easing), and this liquidity-expectation channel produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days as forward guidance and balance-sheet expansion expectations build.
regime_hint: zero lower bound / unconventional policy regime
failure_condition: Mean forward 60d return during ZLB episodes is not statistically greater than the unconditional sample mean (p > 0.05), or fewer than 55% of ZLB episodes show positive forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A severe annualized GDP contraction occurring within a deep recession regime signals sustained earnings compression across broad equity sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate profit forecasts are revised down in line with realized output collapse.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=11525.0 Bn asof t-5mo reflecting deep contraction
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns following GDP contraction triggers are not significantly negative relative to baseline, or the effect disappears once revised (final) GDP estimates are used instead of the initial release.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of extreme labor market slack (high unemployment) and near-zero policy rates indicates monetary accommodation far exceeding standard policy-rule prescriptions, generating expectations of aggressive future stimulus that lift risk appetite and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either variable predicts individually.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=8.1% and FEDFUNDS=0.22% concurrently asof t-1mo
failure_condition: The interaction term (UNRATE x FEDFUNDS threshold condition) shows no incremental predictive power over either single-series baseline, or positive return incidence in triggered periods is not significantly above the unconditional base rate (p > 0.05).
